school.name: The Schoolhouse
school.description: Each teacher at the schoolhouse leads a different class in a different room. Work out who teaches what, and where.
clues: Between English and 96%, one goes with Yearbook and the other with Period 4.
Boyd's grade is higher than the member of the Drama club's grade.
Hale's grade is immediately below the English class' grade.
Between Ames and Chess, one goes with Music and the other with 91%.
Music goes with neither Ames nor Ellis.
Period 5 goes with exactly two of Nash, French, and Yearbook.
The class in Period 2's grade is between the English class' grade and Ames' grade.
Exactly one of these is true: Boyd goes with Choir; English goes with 86%; English goes with Period 4.
Ames, French, 96%, and Period 5 belong to different classes.
Period 1 goes with either Chemistry or French.
91% goes with at least two of Ames, Music, and Drama.

chess.name: The Chess Club
chess.description: Regulars at the chess club each swear by a different opening, a favourite tactic, and a prized set. Work out who plays what.
clues: Between Italian and 2285, one goes with Zwischenzug and the other with 2nd.
Boris' rating is higher than the player who loves the Skewer's rating.
Mikhail's rating is immediately below the Italian player's rating.
Between Anatoly and Fork, one goes with Pirc and the other with 2185.
Pirc goes with neither Anatoly nor Garry.
1st goes with exactly two of Vera, London, and Zwischenzug.
The player who placed 4th's rating is between the Italian player's rating and Anatoly's rating.
Exactly one of these is true: Boris goes with Gambit; Italian goes with 2085; Italian goes with 2nd.
Anatoly, London, 2285, and 1st belong to different players.
5th goes with either French or London.
2185 goes with at least two of Anatoly, Pirc, and Skewer.
